MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arlene Barton, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4127), appearing for defendant / moving party Velan Valve Corp.
Gibert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff/ opposing party Randolph Walton

RULING:
Defendant Velan Valve Corp.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Velan Valve's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the sophisticated intermediary defense (issues 1-3) and granted as to the claims for false representation and punitive damges (issues 4-5). Velan Valve has not established all the elements of a sophisticated intermediary defense. There is no evidence that Velan Valve knew of Chevron's safety precautions such that it would have been reasonable for Velan Valve to rely on Chevron to protect its employees. (Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793.) Velan Valve satisfied its burden of production on the claims for false representation and punitive damages due to plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. Plaintiff concedes that he has not offered any evidence creating a triable issue whether Velan Valve made an affirmative misrepresentation of material fact to plaintiff. Plaintiff has failed to provide admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Velan Valve acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Lertora Aliventi, CSR # 11935
